title: A Free Course on Karl MarxÔÇÖs Capital, Volume 1 from Yale University
description: From Yale pro┬şfes┬şsor Paul North comes a chap┬şter-by-chap┬şter study of Karl MarxÔÇÖs Cap┬şi┬ştal: Cri┬ştique of Polit┬şi┬şcal Econ┬şo┬şmy, Vol┬şume 1. Acco
body:
From Yale pro┬şfes┬şsor Paul North comes a chap┬şter-by-chap┬şter study of Karl MarxÔÇÖs Cap┬şi┬ştal: Cri┬ştique of Polit┬şi┬şcal Econ┬şo┬şmy, Vol┬şume 1. Accord┬şing to the descrip┬ştion that accom┬şpa┬şnies the course on YouTube, this ÔÇťbook from 1872 is still the best guide to the preda┬şto┬şry eco┬şnom┬şic and social sys┬ştem with┬şin which we live. The book solves five basic mys┬şter┬şies in our social world. The mys┬şter┬şies are: why social class┬şes strug┬şgle against one anoth┬şer, why human beings are in the thrall of things, how a quan┬şti┬şty of mon┬şey turns into more mon┬şey with┬şout seem┬şing to add any┬şthing, why some peo┬şple are forced to work and the more they work the less they make pro┬şpor┬ştion┬şal to their effort, and final┬şly, and why it is so hard to trans┬şform the sys┬ştem for the bet┬şter.ÔÇŁ You can watch the 19 lec┬ştures from the course in the playlist above.
Prof. North is the co-edi┬ştor of the new Eng┬şlish trans┬şla┬ştion and crit┬şi┬şcal edi┬ştion of Cap┬şi┬ştal Vol┬şume 1, and itÔÇÖs the text used in the course. If youÔÇÖre inter┬şest┬şed in delv┬şing deep┬şer into MarxÔÇÖs Cap┬şi┬ştal, see the David Har┬şvey cours┬şes list┬şed in the Relat┬şeds below.
This course will be added to our list of 1,700 Free Online Cours┬şes from Top Uni┬şver┬şsi┬şties
Relat┬şed Con┬ştent

title: How KraftwerkÔÇÖs 22-Minute Song ÔÇťAutobahnÔÇŁ Became an Early Masterpiece in Electronic Music (1975)
description:
in History, Music, Technology |   	March 30th, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

It takes about five hours to dri┬şve from D├╝s┬şsel┬şdorf to Ham┬şburg on
body:
in History, Music, Technology |   	March 30th, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

It takes about five hours to dri┬şve from D├╝s┬şsel┬şdorf to Ham┬şburg on the Auto┬şbahn. Dur┬şing that stretch, you can lis┬şten to KraftwerkÔÇÖs album Auto┬şbahn sev┬şen times ÔÇö or if you pre┬şfer, you can loop its epony┬şmous open┬şing song thir┬şteen times. For it was ÔÇťAuto┬şbahn,ÔÇŁ┬ámore so than Auto┬şbahn, that changed the sound of music around the world in ways we still hear today. ÔÇťGer┬şmany was sud┬şden┬şly on the musi┬şcal map,ÔÇŁ writes the┬áGuardianÔÇÖs Tim Jonze. ÔÇťDavid Bowie ÔÇô who used to ride the auto┬şbahn while lis┬şten┬şing to the record ÔÇô moved to Berlin and went on to make the elec┬ştron┬şi┬şcal┬şly influ┬şenced Low, ÔÇťHeroesÔÇŁ and Lodger. Bri┬şan Eno relo┬şcat┬şed to the rur┬şal vil┬şlage of Forst to record with the influ┬şen┬ştial avant-garde band Har┬şmo┬şnia.ÔÇŁ Soon would come the elec┬ştron┬şic pop of Ultra┬şvox, DAF and the Eury┬şth┬şmics, fol┬şlowed by┬áDon┬şna Sum┬şmer and Gior┬şgio MoroderÔÇÖs flood┬şgate-open┬şing ÔÇťI Feel LoveÔÇŁ.
Not a bad pop-cul┬ştur┬şal coup for, as Jonze puts it, ÔÇťa 22-minute 43-sec┬şond song about the Ger┬şman road net┬şwork.ÔÇŁ At the time of its release in ear┬şly 1975, Kraftwerk had put out three full albums, but what would become their sig┬şna┬şture Teu┬şton┬şic-elec┬ştron┬şic sound had┬şnÔÇÖt quite tak┬şen shape. But it was already clear that their work took its inspi┬şra┬ştion from twen┬şti┬şeth-cen┬ştu┬şry moder┬şni┬şty, a sub┬şject of which no sin┬şgle work of man in their home┬şland could have been more evoca┬ştive than the Auto┬şbahn.
With its ori┬şgins in the Weimar Repub┬şlic and its long stretch┬şes with┬şout a speed lim┬şit, the Ger┬şman free┬şway net┬şwork is inter┬şna┬ştion┬şal┬şly regard┬şed as a con┬şcrete sym┬şbol of total per┬şson┬şal free┬şdom, and total per┬şson┬şal respon┬şsi┬şbil┬şi┬şty, with┬şin a high┬şly rule-respect┬şing cul┬şture. To the young mem┬şbers of Kraftwerk, who often drove the D├╝s┬şsel┬şdorf-Ham┬şburg sec┬ştion, it held out the promise of free┬şdom.


	

So did the then-new┬áMin┬şi┬şmoog syn┬şthe┬şsiz┬şer, which cost as much as a Volk┬şswa┬şgen at the time, but offered the chance to make music like noth┬şing the pub┬şlic had ever heard before. ÔÇťAuto┬şbahnÔÇŁ cap┬ştured the imag┬şi┬şna┬ştions of lis┬şten┬şers every┬şwhere with not just its elec┬ştron┬şic effects, but also the incon┬şgruity of their com┬şbi┬şna┬ştion with instru┬şments like the flute (a holdover from KraftwerkÔÇÖs ear┬şli┬şer com┬şpo┬şsi┬ştions) and vehic┬şu┬şlar sounds evoca┬ştive of a gen┬şuine road trip ÔÇö all assem┬şbled at what would then have seemed a hyp┬şnot┬şi┬şcal┬şly expan┬şsive length for a pop song. Lit┬ştle did even the hippest lis┬şten┬şers of the mid-sev┬şen┬şties, such as the Amer┬şi┬şcans tuned into ear┬şly free-form FM sta┬ştions where no cor┬şpo┬şrate pro┬şgram┬şming rules applied, know that they were hear┬şing what Jones calls ÔÇťthe point where elec┬ştron┬şic pop music tru┬şly began.ÔÇŁ All car trips run out of road even┬ştu┬şal┬şly, but human┬şi┬ştyÔÇÖs jour┬şney into the pos┬şsi┬şbil┬şi┬şties of high-tech music shows no signs of approach┬şing its end.
Relat┬şed Con┬ştent:
The Psy┬şche┬şdel┬şic Ani┬şmat┬şed Video for KraftwerkÔÇÖs ÔÇťAuto┬şbahnÔÇŁ (1979)
Kraftwerk Plays a Live 40-Minute Ver┬şsion of their Sig┬şna┬şture Song ÔÇťAuto┬şbahn:ÔÇŁ A Sound┬ştrack for a Long Road Trip (1974)
How Kraftwerk Made the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame
KraftwerkÔÇÖs First Con┬şcert: The Begin┬şning of the End┬şless┬şly Influ┬şen┬ştial Band (1970)
How the Moog Syn┬şthe┬şsiz┬şer Changed the Sound of Music
Hear the Evo┬şlu┬ştion of Elec┬ştron┬şic Music: A Son┬şic Jour┬şney from 1929 to 2019
Based in Seoul,┬áCol┬şin┬áMarshall┬áwrites and broad┬şcasts on cities, lan┬şguage, and cul┬şture. HeÔÇÖs the author of the newslet┬şter┬áBooks on Cities┬áas well as the books┬áÝĽťŕÁş ýÜöýĽŻ ŕŞłýžÇ┬á(No Sum┬şma┬şriz┬şing Korea) and┬áKore┬şan Newtro.┬áFol┬şlow him on the social net┬şwork for┬şmer┬şly known as Twit┬şter at┬á@colinmarshall.

title: Watch Errol MorrisÔÇÖs Tune Out the Noise Free Online: A Documentary About the Financial Revolution That Transformed Investing
description: You canÔÇÖt beat the mar┬şket. That, at least, is the advice we all encounter ear┬şly on when first we try our hand at invest┬şing. Home┬şspun thou
body:
You canÔÇÖt beat the mar┬şket. That, at least, is the advice we all encounter ear┬şly on when first we try our hand at invest┬şing. Home┬şspun though it may sound, the idea has aca┬şd┬şe┬şm┬şic roots: the Effi┬şcient Mar┬şket Hypoth┬şe┬şsis, as the econ┬şo┬şmists call it, holds that the prices in any finan┬şcial mar┬şket already reflect all avail┬şable infor┬şma┬ştion rel┬şe┬şvant to whatÔÇÖs being trad┬şed with┬şin them. In the case of the stock mar┬şket, for exam┬şple, every┬şthing known ÔÇö or indeed, know┬şable ÔÇö about the future prospects of a par┬ştic┬şu┬şlar com┬şpa┬şny is already incor┬şpo┬şrat┬şed into its stock price, or might as well be. If the EMH is true, then it must also be true that nobody can beat the mar┬şket, no mat┬şter how deep their expe┬şri┬şence or devel┬şoped their instinct for pick┬şing stocks.
Nobel Lau┬şre┬şate econ┬şo┬şmist Eugene Fama, whoÔÇÖs done more than any┬şone alive to refine the EFM and keep it in cir┬şcu┬şla┬ştion, appears as one of the inter┬şvie┬şwees in┬áTune Out the Noise, the Errol Mor┬şris-direct┬şed doc┬şu┬şmen┬ştary above. So do a range of oth┬şer fig┬şures, most┬şly sep┬ştu┬şa┬şge┬şnar┬şi┬şan and octo┬şge┬şnar┬şi┬şan, whose great suc┬şcess in their fields owes to their hav┬şing trust┬şed the wis┬şdom of the mar┬şket. All have been involved with the invest┬şment firm Dimen┬şsion┬şal Fund Advi┬şsors, which, since its found┬şing in the ear┬şly nine┬şteen-eight┬şies, has been one of the engines of change in its indus┬ştry. In the first half of the twen┬şti┬şeth cen┬ştu┬şry, invest┬şing had an almost mys┬şti┬şcal qual┬şi┬şty about it ÔÇö a qual┬şi┬şty swept away by the ÔÇťdata rev┬şo┬şlu┬ştionÔÇŁ of the sec┬şond half.
That rev┬şo┬şlu┬ştion was pow┬şered, of course, by com┬şput┬şers. Most of Mor┬şrisÔÇÖ inter┬şvie┬şwees first found them┬şselves placed in front of one of those hulk┬şing, inscrutable machines at some point in their ter┬ştiary edu┬şca┬ştion, more than like┬şly at the Uni┬şver┬şsi┬şty of Chica┬şgo. They learned to work those ear┬şly com┬şput┬şersÔÇÖ punch cards and whirring reels of tape even as elec┬ştron┬şic com┬şput┬şing itself first found its uses in civ┬şi┬şliza┬ştion. Sud┬şden┬şly, though it demand┬şed painstak┬şing col┬şlec┬ştion and pro┬şgram┬şming work, it had become pos┬şsi┬şble to exam┬şine stock mar┬şket data and deter┬şmine what pat┬şterns, if any, it con┬ştained, and whether any investor had con┬şsis┬ştent┬şly out┬şper┬şformed the aver┬şage. The answers revealed would become the premise of not just ÔÇťpas┬şsiveÔÇŁ invest┬şment firms like DFA, but also of the orig┬şi┬şnal cre┬şation of index funds like the S&P 500.
All this may not sound like the usu┬şal ter┬şrain of Errol Mor┬şris, whose pre┬şvi┬şous doc┬şu┬şmen┬ştaries have pro┬şfiled every┬şone from pet ceme┬ştery oper┬şa┬ştors to for┬şmer U.S. sec┬şre┬ştaries of defense to Stephen Hawk┬şing. His films arenÔÇÖt with┬şout their con┬şfronta┬ştion┬şal moments, though giv┬şen that Tune Out the Noise was com┬şmis┬şsioned by DFA itself, it should┬şnÔÇÖt come as a sur┬şprise that Mor┬şris nev┬şer shifts into inter┬şro┬şga┬ştion mode (despite using his sig┬şna┬şture Inter┬şro┬ştron rig to shoot the inter┬şviews). Despite claim┬şing not to know any┬şthing about invest┬şing or finan┬şcial mar┬şkets going in, he finds plen┬şty of over┬şlap with inter┬şests that have long run through his work: epis┬şte┬şmol┬şo┬şgy, for exam┬şple, and the nature of sci┬şen┬ştif┬şic rev┬şo┬şlu┬ştion. After all, most any field has some con┬şnec┬ştion to the┬áinex┬şhaustible sub┬şject of how we know, what we know, and what we canÔÇÖt know. ÔÇťPeo┬şple shrink from uncer┬ştain┬şty, but itÔÇÖs uncer┬ştain┬şty that real┬şly cre┬şates oppor┬ştu┬şni┬şty,ÔÇŁ DFA co-founder David Booth says to Mor┬şris. ÔÇťWhat would the world be like if there were no uncer┬ştain┬şty? I mean, pret┬şty dull.ÔÇŁ
Relat┬şed Con┬ştent:
Nobel Prize-Win┬şning Psy┬şchol┬şo┬şgist Daniel Kah┬şne┬şman (RIP) Explains the Key Ques┬ştion Every Investor Must Ask, and Why ItÔÇÖs a FoolÔÇÖs Errand to Pick Stocks
Errol Mor┬şris Makes His Ground┬şbreak┬şing Series First Per┬şson Free to Watch Online: Binge Watch His Inter┬şviews with Genius┬şes, Eccentrics, Obses┬şsives & Oth┬şer Unusu┬şal Types
Take a Free Course on the Finan┬şcial Mar┬şkets with Robert Shiller, Win┬şner of the Nobel Prize in Eco┬şnom┬şics
ÔÇťThey Were ThereÔÇŁ ÔÇö Errol Mor┬şris Final┬şly Directs a Film for IBM
Under┬şstand┬şing Finan┬şcial Mar┬şkets
Watch A Brief His┬şto┬şry of Time, Errol Mor┬şrisÔÇÖ Film About the Life & Work of Stephen Hawk┬şing
Based in Seoul,┬áCol┬şin┬áMarshall┬áwrites and broad┬şcasts on cities, lan┬şguage, and cul┬şture. HeÔÇÖs the author of the newslet┬şter┬áBooks on Cities┬áas well as the books┬áÝĽťŕÁş ýÜöýĽŻ ŕŞłýžÇ┬á(No Sum┬şma┬şriz┬şing Korea) and┬áKore┬şan Newtro.┬áFol┬şlow him on the social net┬şwork for┬şmer┬şly known as Twit┬şter at┬á@colinmarshall.

title: Art Retreats in the South of France Offer Mirth, Myth, and Mystery
description: The La Napoule Art Foundation is opening its doors more widely than ever before through its new Threshold Art Retreats, a program designed for art
body:
The La Napoule Art Foundation is opening its doors more widely than ever before through its new Threshold Art Retreats, a program designed for artists seeking creative exploration in an extraordinary setting at the Ch├óteau de La Napoule in the south of France. These immersive five-day retreats invite participants of all backgroundsÔÇönot just professional artistsÔÇöto step into a world where art, nature, and personal reflection intersect. With a focus on both artistic practice and inner renewal, the experience offers a rare opportunity to engage deeply with creativity in an ethereal setting shaped by nearly a century of artistic vision.
Each retreat blends hands-on artistic instruction with restorative wellness. Guests might spend their mornings painting in vibrant gardens or working in seaside studios and their afternoons paddleboarding along the Mediterranean or participating in rooftop yoga sessions. Evenings are intentionally reflective, featuring communal meals prepared by the ch├óteauÔÇÖs chef and meditative experiences like sound bowl sessions in the intimate LoversÔÇÖ Tower. The rhythm of each day is designed to inspire both creative output and personal clarity.
Ch├óteau de La Napoule. Photo by Pip Clews
What makes these retreats especially meaningful is access to mentors. Many are led by accomplished artists who were once residents at the ch├óteau themselves, continuing a powerful cycle of artistic exchange. This structure fosters not just skill-building but also a sense of belonging within a creative lineage. Participants are not simply visitors; they become part of an ongoing artistic community rooted in shared inspiration and dialogue.
Importantly, every retreat directly supports the foundationÔÇÖs mission. By opening access to a broader audience, the program helps sustain the ch├óteau and its longstanding artist residency programs. It offers a thoughtful solution to a persistent challenge in the art world: how to make transformative, soul-enriching experiences more accessible while maintaining the historic spaces that make them possible. In this way, participation becomes both personally enriching and purpose-driven.
Artist Vanessa Enriquez. Photo by Afton Love
The setting is also inseparable from the experience. Perched above the Mediterranean, the Ch├óteau de La Napoule feels like a living artwork. Its sculptural windows and doors filter the regionÔÇÖs luminous light, while hybrid figuresÔÇöpart human, part imaginedÔÇöadorn its columns and courtyards that once hosted EuropeÔÇÖs elite. Acres of whimsical gardens enchant, and the Mediterranean sparkles just beyond the ancient ramparts. A sacred world, perched over the sea, entirely devoted to the arts.
This extraordinary place was brought to life by Henry Clews Jr. and Marie Clews, who, nearly 100 years ago, transformed the ruins of an 11th-century fortress into an artistic sanctuary. Henry used the ch├óteau as his canvas, creating sculptures that critiqued ego and vanity with mythic intensity, while Marie designed its ornamental ironwork and geometric gardens. After HenryÔÇÖs early death, Marie carried their shared vision forward, founding the La Napoule Art Foundation in 1950. Their mottoÔÇöÔÇťMirth, Myth, MysteryÔÇŁÔÇöremains etched into the stone, a lasting invitation for others to step inside and be transformed.
The Ch├óteau de La Napoule has always been a place where boundaries dissolve, and lives are rewritten. From HenryÔÇÖs artistic revolution and MarieÔÇÖs preservation efforts, to countless artists finding new directions there, inspirational change seems to be written into its very walls.┬á
Now, that transformation is available to anyone curious enough to seek it.┬á
Artist Anne-Laure Caro. Photo by Laurent Barnavon
La Napoule Art Foundation offers multiple artist residency opportunities throughout the year and operates as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization in the U.S. Threshold Art Retreats hosts immersive programs each summer from June through September. These retreats feature a rotating calendar of specialized creative experiences, including Foraged Books: The Art of Place (artist bookmaking and storytelling), The Human Form in Oil Paints (figure painting from a live model), and Color as Matter: Exploring Pigment Through Surface and Form (discover the physical and symbolic power of color).
Learn more about residencies and upcoming retreats at lnaf.org and thresholdartretreats.com.
Artist Will Clift. Photo by Mathilde France
Henry Clews Studio. Photo by Bruce Keys

schema.headline: Art Retreats in the South of France Offer Mirth, Myth, and Mystery

title: Semiprecious Stones Coat Kathleen Ryan's Oversized Sculptures of Rotting Food
description: There are thousands upon thousands of types of mold out there. Some you can eatÔÇöthink the rind on a wheel of brie or a gray fungus known as ÔÇťnoble
body:
There are thousands upon thousands of types of mold out there. Some you can eatÔÇöthink the rind on a wheel of brie or a gray fungus known as ÔÇťnoble rotÔÇŁ that gives certain types of grapes an extra sweet flavor for dessert wines. But there are plenty we shouldnÔÇÖt eat, and when that loaf of bread in the cupboard begins to turn blue-green, itÔÇÖs definitely time to chuck it in the bin. For Kathleen Ryan, the myriad colors and textures of mold continue to inspire larger-than-life sculptures of fruit and other foods that, in a way, preserve decay.
RyanÔÇÖs oversized works are characterized by their textural finishes, often using salvaged metal and other materials in addition to an array of colored beads and semiprecious stones to achieve the effects of layered fungi and rot. Recent works such as ÔÇťBad Lemon (Slice of Paradise)ÔÇŁ and ÔÇťScrewdriverÔÇŁ nod to the realm of cocktails and, by extension, the notion of luxury and even vacationsÔÇöconcepts that somewhat sour within the context of an increasingly vulnerable economy.
ÔÇťBad Lemon (Slice of Paradise)ÔÇŁ (2024), serpentine, prehnite, azurite in malachite, amazonite, aquamarine, jasper, dolomite, and fuchsite, pyrite, turquoise, labradorite, agate, marble, steel pins on coated polystyrene, Volkswagen fender, 14 x 26 1/2 x 11 1/2 inches
Juxtaposing stones ranging from amethyst and azurite to turquoise and tourmaline with salvaged metal from vintage cars, RyanÔÇÖs sculptures evoke an array of associations. She has previously likened their over-the-top scale to the roadside attractions tourists might see along Interstate highways, such as giant doughnuts and other foods and animals.
Like a geode that doesnÔÇÖt look like much from the outside, works like ÔÇťBad Lemon (Slice of Paradise)ÔÇŁ have two very different personalities, where the metal exterior lets on little about whatÔÇÖs inside. Ryan taps into our appreciation of hidden beauty when opening up an ancient, crystallized stone while simultaneously suggesting the grotesqueness of opening a peach, for example, only to find it rotten inside.
Some of the works seen here were recently on view at Karma in New York, and you can find more on RyanÔÇÖs Instagram.
Detail of ÔÇťBad Lemon (Slice of Paradise)ÔÇŁ
ÔÇťScrewdriverÔÇŁ (2023), onyx, citrine, rhodonite, garnet, agate, tektite, lava rock, turquoise, aquamarine, serpentine, magnesite, amazonite, black tourmaline, jasper, prehnite, ruby in zoisite, marble, amber, labradorite, smoky quartz, quartz, acrylic, steel pins on coated polystyrene, aluminum umbrella, 68ÔÇÖ AMC Javelin trunk, 77 x 88 x 107 inches
Detail of ÔÇťScrewdriverÔÇŁ
ÔÇťBad Lemon (Desert)ÔÇŁ (2023), citrine, jasper, agate, smoky quartz, quartz, carnelian, calcite, labradorite, amber, sunstone, garnet, unakite, red aventurine, tigerÔÇÖs eye, tourmaline, hessonite garnet, chrysoprase, lodolite, lepidolite, serpentine, shell, freshwater pearl, glass, steel pins on coated polystyrene, 16 1/2 x 17 x 14 inches
ÔÇťBad Lemon (Desert)ÔÇŁ
Detail of ÔÇťBad Lemon (Desert)ÔÇŁ
Detail of ÔÇťSunset StripÔÇŁ
Detail of ÔÇťSunset StripÔÇŁ

title: Xiaoze Xie Preserves a Growing Collection of Banned Books in Porcelain
description: Censorship and book bans are on the rise worldwide, prompting growing concerns about access to information and free expression. Although this traj
body:
Censorship and book bans are on the rise worldwide, prompting growing concerns about access to information and free expression. Although this trajectory is increasingly worrisome, it isnÔÇÖt new, as artist Xiaoze Xie reflects on his exhibition In the Name of the Book.
Comprising paintings and life-sized porcelain sculptures, the show encompasses works made in the early 1990s through the present day, all of which reflect on the vital role books play in cultural, political, and social life. XieÔÇÖs practice is largely informed by his upbringing in ChinaÔÇöhe was born in Guangdong the same year as the Cultural RevolutionÔÇö and in 1989, he witnessed the deadly Tiananmen Square protests. After moving to the U.S. in 1993, he began to incorporate this history and concerns about such restrictions into his works as a form of protest. 
ÔÇťThe Forbidden Books Series: The Golden Lotus (Voyeurism); Banned as an obscene book in the 7th year of Qing Emperor TongzhiÔÇÖs reign (1868)ÔÇŁ (2019), porcelain, 12 1/4 x 17 1/4 x 1 3/4 inches
Book banning, particularly in the U.S., can sometimes be framed as a novel issue, and part of XieÔÇÖs effectiveness is that he connects the rise in modern-day censorship to what occurred centuries before. The Forbidden Books Series interprets classic novels, plays, and more that were prohibited largely throughout the Qing Dynasty (1636-1912). Fiction like The Golden Lotus and Water Margin, for example, were charged with being sexually explicit and obscene, while the Chinese government barred the theatrical production The Peony Pavilion from leaving Shanghai for a New York performance in 1998 because of its ÔÇťfeudal, superstitious, and pornographicÔÇŁ qualities.
While these works are well-known cases of censorship, Xie points out that theyÔÇÖre just a sampling of a much larger problem. He writes:

Over the last 2,000 years, the books that have disappeared in China because of prohibition are countless. There is no trace of them anymore; all I have found is a small fraction. All of these old paper stacks, these silent books, consist of thoughts and discourses. These invisible and shapeless things and the stories behind themÔÇöthe complicated contexts of philosophical, religious, political, historical, social, ethical, and racial issuesÔÇöare gone. The history of banning books is a process of challenging repeated oppression and control, and challenging it again. It is alongside this back-and-forth repetition, I think, that history slowly marches on.

Preserving their likeness in porcelain with pages splayed out flat is an act of defiance for the artist, as he presents these otherwise concealed texts as permanently open for public consumption.
In the Name of the Book is on view through April 17 at Sapar Contemporary. Find more from the artist on Instagram.
ÔÇťThe Forbidden Book Series: Water Margin; Banned in the 24th year of Qing Emperor DaoguangÔÇÖs reign and the 7th year of Qing Emperor TongzhiÔÇÖs reign as an obscene bookÔÇŁ (2025), porcelain, painted in underglaze blue, two elements, 8 x 10 1/2 x 1 1/2 inches
ÔÇťThe Forbidden Books Series: The Peony Pavilion (Diagnose evil spirits); Banned in the 24th year of Qing Emperor DaoguangÔÇÖs reign and the 7th year of Qing Emperor TongzhiÔÇÖs reign as an obscene bookÔÇŁ (2024), porcelain, painted in underglaze blue, two elements, 10 7/8 x 11 7/8 x 3/4 inches
ÔÇťThe Forbidden Books Series: The Peony Pavilion (Coming Back to Life)ÔÇŁ (2025), porcelain, painted in underglaze blue, two elements, 10 1/2 x 14 x 1 1/2 inches
ÔÇťThe Forbidden Books Series: Qian Qianyi. ┼Ü┼źraß╣ůgama S┼źtra. Banned in 1770s during the Qianlong Reign/Qing DynastyÔÇŁ (2025), porcelain, unglazed, 12 1/2 x 11 1/2 x 1 1/2 inches

schema.headline: Xiaoze Xie Preserves a Growing Collection of Banned Books in Porcelain

title: April 2026 Opportunities: Open Calls, Residencies, and Grants for Artists
description: Every month, we share opportunities for artists and designers, including open calls, grants, fellowships, and residencies. Make sure you never mis
body:
Every month, we share opportunities for artists and designers, including open calls, grants, fellowships, and residencies. Make sure you never miss out by joining our monthly┬áOpportunities Newsletter.
Earth 2026 Art Awards: Exhibition, Publication, Sales, and Global PromotionFeaturedWhat does your art reveal about Earth? Its beauty, its resilience, or whatÔÇÖs at risk? The 6th edition of Earth 2026 juried awards invites artists worldwide to explore and express the power, beauty, and resilience of our wounded planet as we approach World Earth Day. From nature and climate to human connection and endangered ecosystems, this is your space to turn awareness into art. Selected artists receive an exhibition, Artsy exposure, global promotional materials, catalogue publication, editorial reviews, audience feedback, and other exclusive awards. Learn more and submit: www.exhibizone.com/earthDeadline: 11:59 p.m. PDT April 11, 2026.
The Hopper Prize $4,500 Artist GrantsFeaturedThe Hopper Prize is accepting submissions for $4,500 and $1,000 artist grants. Six grants totaling $13,000 USD are available. Two artists will each receive $4,500, and four artists will each receive $1,000. All media is eligible. Additional exposure is available via a 30-artist shortlist, online journal, and Instagram, currently reaching over 165k.Deadline: 11:59 p.m. PDT May 12, 2026.
Open Calls
Glen Arbor Arts Center: American Tree Open Call┬á(U.S.)American Tree┬áexamines the role trees played in the formation of the United States 250 years ago, and the role they will play moving forward into this nationÔÇÖs next 250 years.┬áSubmissions of two-dimensional and three-dimensional work made between 2024 and 2026 are welcome. There is a $35 application fee for non-GAAC members; itÔÇÖs $25 for members.Deadline: 2 p.m. EDT on April 7, 2026.
Fiber Forward: Open Call for Fiber Art (U.S.)The Gallery at Yellow Studio invites women and non-binary artists to submit fiber-based work that challenges conventional perceptions of the medium. The program seeks innovative approaches that expand fiber artÔÇÖs possibilitiesÔÇöwork that complicates boundaries between decoration and concept, tradition and innovation, or material and meaning. There is a $35 application fee for non-members ($25 for students, free for Yellow Studio members). Fee waivers are available.Deadline: April 8, 2026.
Get Published in Artistonish: Visibility, Engagement, and Sales (International)The┬á69th issue of Artistonish Contemporary Art Magazine, published in April 2026,┬áwill feature a juried selection of contemporary artworks from around the world, showcased online and on premium glossy pages in print. Each selected work is featured with a┬áfull-page image┬áand a┬ádedicated QR code for deeper engagement. Artists will be shared on┬áArtsy, receive a┬ácertificate of achievement┬áand exclusive┬áartwork badges, and┬ábe promoted┬áthrough extensive outreach with lasting online visibility in the global art community. Deadline: 11:59 p.m. PDT, April 9, 2026
Fleurieu Biennale Art Prize (Australia)The Fleurieu Biennale Art Prize will be held┬áat the Fleurieu Arthouse in the heart of McLaren Vale, South Australia, from June 5 to July 5, 2026. A CuratorÔÇÖs Choice exhibition and prize will be held at the Stump Hill Gallery at the McLaren Vale & Fleurieu Coast Visitor Centre in McLaren Vale at the same time. Artists are invited to submit artworks exploring the theme┬áÔÇťA Sense of Place.ÔÇŁ┬áFour prizes total a pool of nearly AUD$30,000.Deadline: April 12, 2026.
First Street Gallery 2026 National Juried Exhibition (U.S.)First Street Gallery invites submissions of two-dimensional works for its summer group show. The galleryÔÇÖs 2026 National Juried Exhibition will be held June 25 to July 18, 2026, in the galleryÔÇÖs New York City Chelsea Art District location. There is a $40 application fee.Deadline: April 15, 2026.
The MACRO Project Billboard Exhibition (State of New York)The MACRO Project invites artists from around New York to submit original artworks for display on billboards on well-trafficked roads in the Finger Lakes region. Four to six artists will be selected to have their work displayed for one month in August or September 2026.Deadline: 11:59 p.m. EDT on April 15, 2026.
Contemporary Craft Textures of Being┬áOpen Call (International)Textures of Being┬ábrings together artists who honor the body as a site of knowledge and creative force grounded in lived experience. This exhibition reimagines disability as a form of freedom and an intimate sensory lens through which the world is perceived and shaped.Deadline: April 30, 2026.
CIFRA Award┬á(International)CIFRA welcomes any form of digital art, and the only condition is that it must be possible to submit as a video. Per the theme ÔÇťRestArt Reality,ÔÇŁ applicants can submit screen-native works that engage with pseudo-evidence, invented archives, alternative chronologies, reconstructions, and ÔÇťfalseÔÇŁ testimonies, and more. Five winners each receive ÔéČ1,000.Deadline: May 8, 2026.
BEERS London Group Exhibitions (International)This is an opportunity for artists working across all media to apply to be a part of a group exhibition at BEERS London. The galleryÔÇÖs objective is to discover new, compelling, and contemporary work to present at its gallery. There is a ┬ú10 application fee.Deadline: June 1, 2026.
21st Edition of Arte Laguna Prize (International)Applying for this unique opportunity to┬áexhibit your work at the Arsenale Nord in Venice.┬áThe finalistsÔÇÖ exhibition will take place from┬áNovember 6 to 29, 2026. The jury will select 120 finalist artists, including the winner of the first prize of ÔéČ10,000, among other awards. The application fee is ÔéČ85.40 (VAT included) for artists under 35 years old and ÔéČ109.80 for those 35 and older.Deadline: June 30, 2026.
Grants
Artadia Awards┬á(New York City)The Artadia Awards provide financial support, exposure, and recognition to artists. The awards are unrestricted, allowing artists to use the funds in any way they choose. A group of finalists facilitates studio visits with the jurors, and a second-round jury then designates three awardees to receive unrestricted funds of $15,000.Deadline: April 1, 2026.
2027 Creative Capital Open Call┬á(U.S.)Creative Capital seeks proposals from individual artists in all 50 states for new artistic works in the visual arts, performing arts, film, and literature. The┬áCreative Capital Award┬áprovides unrestricted project grants of up to $50,000 to individual artists to create new work. The new┬áState of the Art Prize┬áprovides unrestricted artist grants of $10,000.Deadline: April 2, 2026.
Anonymous Was a Woman Environmental Art Grants┬á(U.S.)This program provides grants of up to $20,000 to environmental art projects led by women, transgender, and gender-nonconforming artists in the United States and U.S. Territories.Deadline: 5 p.m. EDT on April 7, 2026.
Woodman Family Foundation Housing Stability Grants (New York City)The Woodman Family Foundation Housing Stability Grant for Artists provides grants of $30,000, distributed over three years, to NYC-based visual artists in need who are seeking support for stable housing. In its second cycle, the foundation will award grants to five artists.Deadline: April 14, 2026.
Textile Society of America Research Travel Grants┬á(U.S.)The TSA Textile Research Travel Grant supports TSA members traveling to conduct textile-focused research. This may include visiting museum collections, engaging with artists or weavers in their local environments, studying special exhibitions, or other research opportunities centered on the study of actual textiles. Applicants may request┬á$500 to $1,000, depending on the scope of travel.Deadline: April 15, 2026.
CCA Islands Travel Scholarships to Japan┬á(International)The CCA Travel Scholarship is for emerging artists, curators, architects, designers, and those who aim to be a professional in various areas of contemporary art and culture, pursuing their research and creative activities in Japan. The scholarship offers research expenses of JPY 200ÔÇÖ000 (about $1,250) and covers one return transportation fare to and from Japan. Preference is given to applicants under 35 years old.Deadline: April 24, 2026.
3pts Artists & Makers Impact Fund (U.S.)3pts awards $3,500 to U.S.-based artists and makers who create tangible objects or goods and are working to sustain and grow a business. Funding may be used for materials, equipment, workspace costs, professional development, production expenses, or other needs that strengthen an artist or makerÔÇÖs practice.Deadline: 11:59 p.m. EDT on April 27, 2026.
Pentimenti Emerging Filmmakers Grant(U.S.)The Pentimenti Grant for Emerging Filmmakers endeavors to support work by up-and-coming women, non-binary, and/or LGBTQ+ filmmakers. In keeping with PentimentiÔÇÖs mission, the project must pertain to art or artists in some way, although it doesnÔÇÖt necessarily need to be a documentary. The recipient will receive $2,000, consultations with Pentimenti staff, and a spotlight feature on PentimentiÔÇÖs communication channels.Deadline: April 30, 2026.
The Adolf and Esther Gottlieb Emergency Grant┬á(International)This program provides one-time financial assistance to qualified painters, printmakers, and sculptors whose needs resulted from an unforeseen catastrophic incident and who lack the resources to meet that situation. Awardees typically receive $5,000 and up to $15,000.Deadline: Rolling.
Pollock-Krasner Foundation Grant┬á(International)The foundation welcomes applications from painters, sculptors, and artists working on paper, including printmakers. Grants are intended for one year and range up to $50,000. The artistÔÇÖs circumstances determine the size of the grant, and professional exhibition history will be considered.Deadline: Rolling.
Residencies, Fellowships, & More
Peninsula School of Art Time & Space Residencies┬á(International)Designed for artists of all career stages, this residency program at Peninsula School of Art in Fish Creek, Wisconsin, is an immersive studio experience awarded to six individuals per year. The program prioritizes time and space for its residents to engage fully in their creative practice for six weeks, creating uninterrupted opportunities to experiment and pursue new projects and ideas. Residencies are free of charge. There is a $10 application fee.Deadline: April 1, 2026.
BRIClab Contemporary Art Residency┬á(New York City)BRIClab invites applications for its contemporary art residency. Participating artists receive a stipend of $2,500 in addition to access to BRICÔÇÖs studios, media equipment, and materials, cohort gatherings, and more. Deadline: April 3, 2026.
Northern Clay Center Early Career Residencies (International)This residency program encompasses two fellowshipsÔÇöThe Anonymous Artist Studio Fellowship and the BIPOC Studio FellowshipÔÇödesigned to provide up to three ceramic artists with the opportunity to be in residence at Northern Clay Center for one year, where they can develop their work and exchange ideas and knowledge with other ceramic artists.Deadline: 5 p.m. CDT on April 5, 2026.
Delfina Foundation Open Call (India)In partnership with┬áThe Charles Wallace India Trust and The Inlaks Shivdasani Foundation, Delfina Foundation welcomes applications from contemporary visual artists from India for a residency in London. This residency will take place from January 5 to March 28 during Delfina FoundationÔÇÖs 2027 winter residency season.Deadline: April 5, 2026.
Delfina Foundation Open Call (Latin America, the Caribbean, and related diasporic communities in the U.S.)In partnership with El Espacio 23 / Jorge M. P├ęrez Collection, Delfina Foundation is pleased to announce an open call for Latin American and Caribbean artists based in those regions or from related diasporic communities living in the U.S. Two artists will be selected for fully funded 12-week residencies.Deadline: April 6, 2026.
Queer and Trans Research Lab Artist-in-Residence (International)The Queer and Trans Research Lab (QTRL) at the University of Toronto invites qualified applicants to apply to be an artist-in-residence┬áfor the 2026-27 school year. QTRL provides financial and other material support for artists working in any medium whose work centers on LGBTQ2S+ lives, communities, histories, and cultures. The residency will culminate in┬áa funded exhibition, reading, screening, or performance of the residentÔÇÖs work-in-progress.Deadline: April 6, 2026.
Walker Youngbird FoundationÔÇÖs Native Neon (U.S.)Native Neon is a brand new residency offering hands-on instruction, collaborative fabrication, and full production support for Indigenous artists to explore neon for the first time. Residents receive a $10,000 stipend, lodging, round-trip travel, training, and more.Deadline: 11:59 p.m. on April 7, 2026.
Delfina Foundation Open Call with St PaulÔÇÖs Cathedral for Artists from Latin America and the Caribbean┬á(International)With support from St. PaulÔÇÖs Cathedral and The Irma Pastrana Borrero Foundation for HumanityÔÇÖs Peace and Development, Delfina Foundation welcomes applications from contemporary visual artists based in Latin America and the Caribbean for a residency in London. The selected artist will also be ÔÇťin residenceÔÇŁ at St. PaulÔÇÖs Cathedral and will use the residency to explore the relationship between space and spirituality.Deadline: April 12, 2026.
Lyc├ęe Fran├žais de Chicago Artist-in-Residence Program(International)Every other year, the LFCÔÇÖs Artist-in-Residence program welcomes a creator who wishes to develop an artistic project in a school setting. For the 2026-27 resident, LFC encourages applications from artists working in ceramics and/or textiles. The residency runs from four to 12 weeks, and the artist will receive a total stipend of $15,000 from the LFC to cover all expenses.Deadline: April 15, 2026.
Dome House Al & Mickey Quinlan Artist Residency Program┬á(Midwest)The residency, organized by The Miller Art Museum, invites artists to WisconsinÔÇÖs┬áDoor Peninsula┬áfor eight weeks at the Dome House, a unique concrete structure built into sand dunes abutting Whitefish Dunes State Park on the shores of Lake Michigan. Artists from Illinois, Indiana, Iowa, Kansas, Michigan, Minnesota, Missouri, Nebraska, North Dakota, Ohio, South Dakota, and Wisconsin are welcome to apply.Deadline: April 17, 2026.
BRIClab Video Art Residency┬á(New York City)BRIClab invites applications for its video art residency. Participating artists receive a stipend of $2,500 in addition to 80 hours of BRICÔÇÖs community media studios and editing suites, plus one-on-one production support and consultations with an advisor and/or BRIC curatorial staff.Deadline: April 17, 2026.
La Napoule Art Foundation Fall Residency┬á(International)La Napoule Art Foundation offers an opportunity for artists to engage in meaningful work and cultural interchange at the Ch├óteau de La Napoule, located just outside Cannes, France.┬áFall residency dates are October 3 to November 6, 2026, and artists working in any disciplineÔÇövisual artists, musicians, dancers, writers, etc.ÔÇöare welcome to apply. Selected artists receive a┬átravel and material stipend, room and board, studio space, and time and space to create in an awe-inspiring locale.Deadline: April 20, 2026.
The Martin House Creative Residency Program┬á(U.S.)This competitive program is open to applicants who seek the resources to support┬áongoing projects┬áor the┬ácreation of new work, with special emphasis on the Frank Lloyd Wright-designed Martin House. Creative makers who are selected to participate will generally spend two to four weeks on site. Residents will receive a stipend of┬á$5,000, and travel expenses of up to $1,000┬áwill also be provided to residents from outside New YorkÔÇÖs Buffalo-Niagara region.Deadline: April 24, 2026.
BEERS London 40-Day Residency (International)The residency supports artists for a dedicated period of creative development in London, along with professional support and creative outreach. For 40 days, selected artists will receive studio space, an honorarium for living and materials,┬águidance from the BEERS team, and the freedom to shape their own working schedule. Applications remain active for 12 months, after which point artists can reapply. Current residency periods fall within May to July 2026 and January to March 2027. There is a ┬ú20 application fee.Deadline: Rolling.

schema.headline: April 2026 Opportunities: Open Calls, Residencies, and Grants for Artists
